meta.name: Health & Safety
meta.description: govOS free public service: a plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 8 (Health & Safety) — nuisances, sanitation, noise, solid waste, and enforcement. Sourced to Municode + County; first version, intentionally structural where corpus is pending; expanding.
meta.pageTitle: Title 8 — Health & Safety · govOS · Kilo Aupuni
meta.eyebrow: 12 Stones Global · Kilo Aupuni · govOS · public service

hero.lead: A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 8 (Health & Safety) — the County's nuisance, sanitation, noise, and solid-waste rules, and how violations are enforced. Solid waste is run by the Department of Environmental Management. This is a structural first version — much of Title 8 is expanding as the ingestion corpus is sourced verbatim; we assert only what is cited; nothing here is legal advice.

sections.title: ★ Parcel lookup
sections.note: Many health-and-safety matters (overgrown lots, illegal dumping, sanitation nuisances) are tied to a property. Enter a Maui County TMK to see the parcel's designations from the Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS as context before reporting or responding to a nuisance.

source.html: Live designations: Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS. No parcel data is stored or fabricated — fetched live.

sections.title: 1 · What Title 8 covers
sections.note: A structural overview while chapters are sourced verbatim. We name only what is in the Code's own structure.
sections.html: Title 8 (Health & Safety, §§8.04.005–8.34.080) carries the County's public-health, sanitation, nuisance, noise, and solid-waste rules. Solid waste — collection, transfer stations, the landfill, and illegal-dumping rules — is administered by the Department of Environmental Management, Solid Waste Division. The chapter-by-chapter content is expanding as the ingestion session sources each chapter from Municode; until then, use the Municode link for authority.

source.html: Maui County Code Title 8 (Health & Safety) + County Solid Waste Division.
source.expanding: per-chapter content (nuisance, noise, sanitation, solid waste)

sections.title: 2 · Common Title 8 matters & where to go
sections.note: Where residents typically interact with Title 8.

rows.v: County noise rules (+ State noise code)

source.html: County of Maui Solid Waste Division + MCC Title 8.

sections.title: 3 · Enforcement
sections.note: Health-and-safety violations are reachable through the County enforcement engine. Not legal advice.
sections.html: Title 19's enforcement chapter, §19.530.030, expressly reaches violations of MCC titles 8, 12, 14, 16, 18, 19 and 20 plus related rules and permits — so a Title 8 health-and-safety violation (e.g. an unsanitary nuisance or illegal dumping) can draw a notice of violation and order requiring abatement and/or civil fines, final after 30 days unless appealed to the Board of Variances and Appeals.

source.html: Maui County Code Ch. 19.530 (§19.530.030 reach, sourced in the Title 19 service). Note: the §15-2/MC-15 cross-title fine schedule is specific to Titles 16·18·19 and does not extend to Title 8.

sections.title: 4 · Who pays — the charter lens
sections.note: Labeled analysis — health & safety read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.
sections.mauiCharter: Under the Maui County Charter, the County protects public health and safety through sanitation, nuisance, and solid-waste programs administered by Environmental Management; fees (e.g. tipping fees) and the general fund support the service.
sections.sovereignCharter: Under the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, a clean, healthy living environment is a shared birthright; protecting it is a communal kuleana. The lens favors accessible disposal and strong action against polluters, and resists fees so high they push residents toward illegal dumping.

position.label: ⚖️ Position — analysis, not law
position.text: Keep legal disposal easy and affordable so the clean path is the cheap path, and aim enforcement at those who dump on the commons. Same title, read two ways.

sections.text: Free now (no account): this page, the parcel lookup, and the structural overview are all Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): a nuisance-report helper and an AI Title-8 assistant will be verification-gated via the free govOS beta portal (identity verified with Stripe Identity — free, no charge); only verified-status is stored, never the raw ID. No secret keys (environment references only).
